place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
building's exterior may look unassuming or even a lil spooky but the inside is really cozy and homely! lovely space, limited tables but plenty of seats

if you really want a table, they have a table booking system you can use to ensure you have one when you get there!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Nostalgia a plenty in this well preserved library that houses many childhood memories for some.

Located in the quiet side of Queenstown facing sky residences @ Dawson, short walk from Queenstown MRT, a cozy library to spend your time here.

2 levels served by a lift & stairs, lots of reading areas and I particularly enjoy the level 2 quiet reading room facing the greenery.

There are lots of nostalgia and nice touches to this library.

Take your time to explore, read, borrow or exchange. what a privilege!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As of April 2026: It is a old school library with the kinda of nostalgia you get when you visited a library in the 2000's.

Most of the Library's floor space is dedicated to books with working space being rather limited. It's not very convenient to access either as you gotta walk abit from the MRT station and not much amenities nearby.

The tables tend to get booked out until like 7pm ish. With patrons playing musical chairs where someone who has a booking asks those who didn't make a booking to move.

Interesting mix of patrons, with elderly men being the majority and somehow quite a few expats.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC)
Review on 20/1/2026

The Singapore Recreation Club (SRC), founded in 1883 and set along the iconic Padang, is one of Singapore’s most historic social‑sports institutions. Its clubhouse offers a wide range of sports, recreation, dining, and event facilities that continue to serve a vibrant and diverse community.

The club’s most striking feature is its grand entrance, framed by two sweeping curved staircases. This graceful, ceremonial design creates a sense of ascending into a place of heritage and dignity. Against the backdrop of the Padang and the Civic District, the entrance stands out as a timeless and memorable architectural landmark.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Private club in central district with roofed warm and cold swimming pool, restaurant, bar with live band and cafe.
Cost environment for private gathering and easily accessible by MRT via City Hall station

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Dinner with an old friend at the SRC'S Chinese Restaurant. Reminiscent of the old days in Singapore, with traditions alive and well. Very pleasant evening 😊😋

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Our family had a wonderful visit to the Singapore Recreation Club (SRC) during their open house! The location right by the Padang is fantastic, and we were impressed that the facilities are truly top-notch. The pool looked inviting, and the entire club was clean and prestigious. It's clear why it's a desirable spot, but be aware that it is indeed for members only. We really enjoyed our temporary visit and appreciated seeing the high quality of the club.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Not a member but, their facility is quite complete. From dance, restaurant, chill drinking club, outside chill area, car parking, swimming pool, gym, to pickleball court

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text:
Our 14-month-old son absolutely loves his weekly Play and Bloom class and looks forward to it every week. He has thrived in their care, growing more confident and engaged through their perfectly tailored activities.

The teachers are truly wonderful—they are so patient, loving, and skilled at connecting with each child. A special highlight was the Christmas party. We were so impressed by the atmosphere; the entire place was genuinely filled with the joy and happiness of the children and their parents. It was a beautiful, festive event that everyone enjoyed.

Agora has created a warm, stimulating, and community-focused environment. We couldn't be happier!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text:
I’ve had a really positive experience with the EP coordinators. They take the time to recommend classes that truly suit each child’s level, and I really appreciate how responsive they are—especially when it comes to arranging makeup classes quickly when needed.

I also feel reassured knowing that the vendors in Agora are carefully screened, so only reputable and trustworthy providers are included.

What stands out to me most is the regular check-ins with both parents and children. It really helps me understand my child’s progress and any concerns along the way. The coordinators often go beyond their roles, and I feel they genuinely care about providing holistic support and guidance for the kids.

One small suggestion from my side would be to consider incentives for longer-term or multiple EP sign-ups. It would also be really helpful if there were better lunch arrangements in Agora, especially for students with classes starting at 2:45pm, so they have enough time to eat and rest properly before lessons. A more substantial lunch option (beyond the usual student care catering) would make the experience even better.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text:
My son has had an amazing journey with Agora Preschool right from his play school days to preschool. He made many wonderful friends, learned so many new things, and developed good manners and positive behavior along the way. He truly enjoyed coming to school every day and has grown beautifully under the care and guidance of his teachers.

We will always miss the school, his friends, and all the wonderful teachers who made his learning journey so special and memorable. Thank you for everything and for being such an important part of his early years.

Special thanks to Ms Claire for being super support and flexible throughout 🙏

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text: My child had a hard P1 transition and had a rough Q1 with school + post school care before moving to Agora. Now she is thriving in P2 and attends 4 enrichment classes with Agora. During her stay, Ms Linda fr sch care was wonderful with helping an anxious and change resistant stranger fearing child adapt and integrate to make new friends at a managed pace. Yenn fr enrichment has been an awesome parent support. She arranges enrichment, make ups, checks up on kid, intervenes when my child/ kids arrives disregulated and in tears. She's always sitting there, always comforts her and is able partner enrichment teachers to ease my kid into classes on bad days (which was half of P1). Missed class refunds are arranged automatically, new interesting classes are sent for consideration. As a working parent, I can focus and I know my kid is well cared for at the center and spelling/ ting Xie is revised in Agora. I can reach out to Yenn anytime and the team is responsive and attentive to parents and kids needs. Thank you so much :)

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: Agora Colearning (Preschool, Play School, Enrichment, Student Care)
review_text: My 2 kids are currently enrolled in Agora Colearning for their enrichment lessons.We love the experience and the interactions with the staff and teachers.I would like to specially compliment Ms Yen for her friendly, efficient and dedicated help thus far. She is always ready to attend to my queries promptly and make us feel so warm and welcoming when my kids have their lessons at the centre. The teachers, Ms Xing, Coach Charles and Tr Beth are wonderful teachers, always giving my kids guidance and encouragements.We are so grateful to have them to coach our kids through their education journey and looking forward to many more years with Agora!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: One of the best national libraries I’ve seen, with a large number of books to rent. 24/7 book drop, a lot of magazines available to read, plenty of quiet, comfy and beautiful areas to read in, and that’s all just the basement level – there are 15 floors all with their own content. Floors 5, 10 and 15 have green areas if you’d like to be in a forest-like environment while reading/working/studying. If you’re nearby, surely a great place to visit!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Perhaps the largest public library in Singapore. Hard to find a librarian for assistance, and bookings for seats are required if you do not want to be asked to leave just because someone has booked the seats. A really stupid concept for those who would casually come into the library if they are in the area. Throughout the years, NLB has done nothing to enhance the library experience. What the public has been slapped with are rules and regulations which makes visiting the library a chore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Came purely for the kids section of the library and it sure lived up to our expectations. The little one thoroughly enjoyed himself amidst the aquarium themed library and the endless literature around the area. Very thoughtfully, they also offer floor cushions where you can take and just find a spot to read and chill out on. The hit area with the deck chairs is the central of the kids section but was unfortunately pretty much always filled with people. Great place to spend the weekend letting the kids immerse themselves. Parking is also pretty reasonable at $1.20 per hour on Sundays.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The Central Public Library in Singapore is a vibrant space blending innovation, design, and functionality. Located at the National Library Building, it offers an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and themed spaces catering to all age groups. Highlights include the eco-friendly My Tree House, the world’s first green library for children, and its lush ambiance. The library’s modern facilities, cozy reading nooks, and seamless technology make it an ideal place for study or leisure. Regular workshops and events add to its community spirit. A hub of knowledge and creativity, it’s a must-visit for book lovers and lifelong learners.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Today, I attended Emma Memma's Performance at Drama Centre. I grew up watching Sesame Street and Hi-5, and of course, The Wiggles. So when I learned that Emma Memma, formerly of The Wiggles, was performing, I was really excited.

What I find especially meaningful is the way Emma Memma and Elvin Melvin connects through sign language. For that, kudos to them.

Some children can only sing and communicate through sign language — yet they’re often overlooked in theatre shows. That’s why Emma Memma’s performances stand out: she reminds us that inclusion matters, and every child deserves to shine. (Catch her in action on youtube.)

Drop by our libraries or check out the NLB online portal and mobile app! You’ll find a huge variety of e-books, e-magazines, audiobooks, and lots of other exciting features. Truly one of the best resources you can enjoy in Singapore 🙂

Have you seen the sky-bridged library, on the upper floors, rising 16 storeys high? Designed with floor-to-ceiling glass panels, it welcomes natural light into every corner, creating a warm, open atmosphere. Look out for the greenery that softens the sleek architecture, blending nature with structure to offer a calming, inviting space.

Floors 5, 10 and 15 have green areas if you’d like to be in a forest-like environment while reading/working/studying. If you’re nearby, surely a great place to visit!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Perhaps the largest public library in Singapore. Hard to find a librarian for assistance, and bookings for seats are required if you do not want to be asked to leave just because someone has booked the seats. A really stupid concept for those who would casually come into the library if they are in the area. Throughout the years, NLB has done nothing to enhance the library experience. What the public has been slapped with are rules and regulations which makes visiting the library a chore.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Came purely for the kids section of the library and it sure lived up to our expectations. The little one thoroughly enjoyed himself amidst the aquarium themed library and the endless literature around the area. Very thoughtfully, they also offer floor cushions where you can take and just find a spot to read and chill out on. The hit area with the deck chairs is the central of the kids section but was unfortunately pretty much always filled with people. Great place to spend the weekend letting the kids immerse themselves. Parking is also pretty reasonable at $1.20 per hour on Sundays.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: The Central Public Library in Singapore is a vibrant space blending innovation, design, and functionality. Located at the National Library Building, it offers an extensive collection of books, digital resources, and themed spaces catering to all age groups. Highlights include the eco-friendly My Tree House, the world’s first green library for children, and its lush ambiance. The library’s modern facilities, cozy reading nooks, and seamless technology make it an ideal place for study or leisure. Regular workshops and events add to its community spirit. A hub of knowledge and creativity, it’s a must-visit for book lovers and lifelong learners.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Today, I attended Emma Memma's Performance at Drama Centre. I grew up watching Sesame Street and Hi-5, and of course, The Wiggles. So when I learned that Emma Memma, formerly of The Wiggles, was performing, I was really excited.

What I find especially meaningful is the way Emma Memma and Elvin Melvin connects through sign language. For that, kudos to them.

Some children can only sing and communicate through sign language — yet they’re often overlooked in theatre shows. That’s why Emma Memma’s performances stand out: she reminds us that inclusion matters, and every child deserves to shine. (Catch her in action on youtube.)

Drop by our libraries or check out the NLB online portal and mobile app! You’ll find a huge variety of e-books, e-magazines, audiobooks, and lots of other exciting features. Truly one of the best resources you can enjoy in Singapore 🙂

Have you seen the sky-bridged library, on the upper floors, rising 16 storeys high? Designed with floor-to-ceiling glass panels, it welcomes natural light into every corner, creating a warm, open atmosphere. Look out for the greenery that softens the sleek architecture, blending nature with structure to offer a calming, inviting space.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Visited the National Library Building mainly for The Albatross File: Singapore’s Independence Declassified exhibition, and it turned out to be a meaningful experience. The exhibition itself is relatively small, but the historical significance is immense. It provides a fascinating and candid look into the events leading up to Singapore’s separation from Malaysia in 1965, including documents and notes showing that both sides had, by then, come to see separation as the likely path forward. Seeing the actual letters, transcripts, and handwritten notes made this pivotal chapter of Singapore’s history feel very real and personal

I also took some time to roam around the Lee Kong Chian Reference Library and was impressed by how incredibly well managed the entire place is. The building itself is architecturally striking, modern, spacious, and filled with natural light. The library environment feels calm, organized, and conducive for learning and research, with clear signage and well-maintained facilities throughout

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The National Library is conveniently located with good traffic access from around the island. It’s a comfy cool place to be if your want to study, research and just reading. You may spent hours but no worries about hunger as the are many choices of food in the area.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text: The Albatross File exhibition at NLB is a fascinating showcase of Singapore’s separation history, featuring declassified documents, oral histories, and immersive multimedia displays. It brings to life the tense years leading up to independence, blending archival records with dramatized storytelling.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
2026 January - Did quick look around, enjoyed reading the stories from the mini exhibition Knots of Time. Looks like there are other exhibitions and all sorts of collection, will be back to explore and read.

Great ambience, nice for researchers and the public alike. Place has plenty of space if you'd like to get some work done, offers brilliant views, and even has a skygarden. Power and water is readily available, and theres even sufficient space for a stroll if you're feeling a mental block coming on.

Will be back to check out the albatross files. The atrium at the bottom is very pleasant, and the vaguely 'heritage' old chang kee seems like a good place to check out as well.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: National Library / Lee Kong Chian Reference Library
review_text:
Albatross exhibition of Singapore

Thankful for this curated history that bares all that Singaporeans may not know. We had the privilege of having seniors in their 70s who came along, cementing our understanding of what they had gone thru.

The whole setup allowed us to go at our own pace and the voice of our late and retired ministers add a sense of urgency and realism.

A suggestion for those who are not English educated, as the ministers are speaking in English, please allow translator set made avail in various language to our non English speaking seniors.

Lastly the wooden board is super heavy. Made it very difficult for us to lift up the board to read the files.  Lastly is there a need to have the board hidden? Most don’t know it exist until someone lifted it up.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly an amazing place and definitely a must-visit attraction in Singapore. The entire area is beautifully designed, extremely clean, and very well maintained, making it a wonderful experience for visitors of all ages.

The Flower Dome and Cloud Forest offer a unique opportunity to see plants and flowers from different parts of the world. During our visit, we also experienced the Tulip Feast, where we saw many beautiful varieties of tulips in full bloom. The colorful display was absolutely stunning and added a vibrant touch to the overall experience. The indoor waterfall inside the Cloud Forest is especially impressive and creates a refreshing atmosphere. The Supertree Grove is another major highlight, and the night light show there feels absolutely magical with the music and colorful lights.

The whole place is spacious and easy to explore, although there is a lot to walk around, so it can feel a little tiring after some time. Still, the beautiful views and attractions make the walking completely worth it.

There are many perfect photo spots throughout the gardens, especially during the evening when the night view looks stunning. Souvenir shops are also available if you would like to buy gifts or memories from your visit.

Overall, it’s a peaceful and beautiful place to relax, enjoy nature, and experience one of Singapore’s most iconic attractions. Highly recommended, especially for first-time visitors.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is truly a highlight of Singapore and absolutely not to be missed. I’d highly recommend getting a combo ticket for the Cloud Forest and Flower Dome—plan for about 2-3 hours to cover both.

The Cloud Forest is spectacular, almost Jurassic Park-like in parts, and enjoyable for both kids and adults. There’s misting at specific times, so it’s worth checking those in advance. A great way to explore is to head to the top level first and then make your way down, stopping at the different exhibits along the way.
The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, showcasing flowers and plants from around the world, with plenty of photo-worthy corners throughout. There’s also a nice gift shop with garden and flower-themed souvenirs.

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

I also got a separate ticket for the OCBC Skyway and it was 100% worth it. I went up right at 7:45 PM during the light and sound show and got to watch it from the top—an incredible experience. You can stay up for about 15 minutes, and while there’s an elevator, lines to go can get long.

For food, the Jurassic-themed food court nearby is a good option for dinner. Overall, it felt like a well-spent ~$60 to explore all three places. I’d suggest going after 3 PM and spending the entire evening there to fully enjoy the whole area.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Gardens by the Bay is absolutely magical and one of my favorite spots in Singapore. The Supertree Grove is stunning, especially at night when the light and sound show begins—it feels like stepping into a fairy tale. I loved walking through the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest; both are beautifully maintained and full of unique plants and flowers from around the world. The pathways and gardens are perfect for leisurely strolls, and there are so many spots to take photos or just pause and enjoy the scenery. It’s peaceful, inspiring, and feels like a green oasis right in the middle of the city. Whether you’re a nature lover, a photography enthusiast, or just looking for a relaxing escape, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
During my recent visit to Singapore, one of the most memorable places I explored was Gardens by the Bay. This remarkable attraction is a perfect example of how nature, technology, and sustainability can come together to create something truly extraordinary.

From the moment I entered the gardens, I was impressed by the sheer scale and beauty of the place. The landscape is meticulously maintained, featuring a stunning variety of plants, flowers, and themed gardens from around the world. The iconic Supertree Grove is undoubtedly the highlight, with its futuristic structures creating a breathtaking skyline, especially during the evening when they are beautifully illuminated.

I also had the opportunity to visit the conservatories, where the climate-controlled environments showcase unique plant species from different regions. Walking through these attractions felt like traveling across multiple ecosystems in a single day. The attention to detail, cleanliness, and visitor-friendly facilities were outstanding.

What impressed me most was Singapore’s commitment to environmental sustainability, which is reflected throughout the entire garden. Despite being located in the heart of a modern city, the gardens provide a peaceful escape where visitors can relax, take photographs, and appreciate nature.

Whether you are traveling with family, friends, or exploring solo, Gardens by the Bay is a must-visit destination. It offers a unique blend of natural beauty, innovation, and world-class design that leaves a lasting impression. My visit was truly unforgettable, and I would highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to Singapore.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Gardens by the Bay is one of Singapore’s most impressive attractions and a place that keeps rewarding repeat visits. What started as a quick visit to the Supertrees has grown into an entire day of exploration, from the Flower Dome and Cloud Forest to the outdoor gardens, lakes, sculptures, and hidden corners scattered throughout the grounds

The Cloud Forest remains the highlight for me. Walking through the cool mist, towering ferns, orchids, and prehistoric-looking plants feels like stepping into another world. The elevated walkways offer fantastic perspectives, and the constantly changing displays make every visit feel fresh. The Flower Dome is equally beautiful, with seasonal floral exhibitions and meticulously maintained landscapes

Beyond the conservatories, there is so much to discover. The Supertrees are iconic, the outdoor gardens are peaceful and surprisingly diverse, and even simple walks around the lakes reveal interesting plants, wildlife, and photo opportunities. I particularly enjoyed wandering through areas that many visitors overlook, including the lily-covered ponds and the themed garden displays

Another pleasant surprise was discovering that the Conservatory Gift Shop leads into a complimentary Jurassic World Experience, which added an unexpected element of fun to the visit. Combined with convenient access via MRT, dining options such as Satay by the Bay, excellent facilities, and world-class landscaping, it is easy to spend an entire day here without getting bored

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Despite feedback done couple of months ago , issue still remains the same . Passengers rushing to the lift without giving priority to wheelchair passengers while staffs are seen chatting with each other / resting in the station control .

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Bencoolen MRT Station (DT21) on the Downtown Line is a clean, modern, and well-maintained station. Its strategic location near cultural and educational spots like the National Museum and SMU makes it a convenient stop for students, tourists, and office workers. The station is known for its deep underground structure, one of the deepest in Singapore, but escalators and lifts ensure accessibility. With clear signage and a generally smooth passenger flow, commuting here is hassle-free. However, exits are limited compared to larger interchange stations.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Tucked within the vibrant heart of Tekka Food Centre, 蒸好味 Steamed Fish Cuisine is a quiet ode to the beauty of honest, well-executed cooking.

What sets this humble gem apart is its unwavering commitment to freshness — each fish is live-caught for the day, delivering a delicate sweetness that simply cannot be replicated. Gently steamed to perfection, the flesh remains tender and succulent, elevated only by a refined touch of ginger and scallion that enhances rather than overpowers. Every bite speaks of clarity, balance, and respect for the ingredient.

Equally noteworthy is their steamed egg — a silky, comforting masterpiece crafted from real eggs cracked fresh on the spot, not pre-mixed liquid substitutes. The result is a custard-like texture that is impossibly smooth, rich, and deeply satisfying.

In a setting as bustling as Tekka, this stall stands out not through extravagance, but through precision, sincerity, and an unwavering dedication to quality. A true hidden indulgence for those who appreciate the quiet luxury of simplicity done exceptionally well.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
The food never disappoints—every bite hits the spot. While the service is decent, there’s still room to elevate the overall experience. If you want to enjoy it at its best, come before lunchtime… unless you don’t mind a long, hungry wait in line.
And once you’re seated, don’t hold back—order the duck webs, pig ears, and duck wings. Paired with an ice-cold beer, it’s the kind of combo that keeps you reaching for more.
One visit is all it takes—you’ll already be planning your next.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The first time I tried the food stalls here was on a Sunday afternoon. It was crowded and I got overwhelmed. I came back on a Wednesday and had tremendous service even though we barely spoke the same language.  Food was delicious. I found a clean spot to sit and eat and soak in the atmosphere.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Actually went there to try again the famous 545 whampoa prawn noodle. Saw this stall (several stall from 545). An old amah alone selling. Surprisingnya the food taste good. And worth the price for a bowl like this.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Did a market walk at Tekka Centre — a true slice of Singapore life! 🇸🇬

First built in 1915 as Kandang Kerbau Market, this Little India landmark has evolved into a vibrant multi-use hub:

Ground/Level 1 – bustling dry/wet market & food centre with fresh produce & multi-ethnic eats - spoilt for choice!

Level 2 – heritage shops with colourful textiles & hidden gems

B1 – convenient parking below

My Favorite stop: Alhajar Latifa (#01-116) — where curry spices are freshly blended just for you. Always greeted by cheerful & pleasant staff.

Will be back to explore more of Level 2 soon!😻

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Plaza Singapura is very crowded but extremely useful. I had multiple skin and hair treatments here and got excellent results was 99.99.9% effective. You can trust the professionals here; they deliver exactly what they promise.😎

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Sleek and modern mall with immense verticality, spanning up towards the 7th storey. Each floor especially the upper ones are not that large hence it's quite easy to navigate, it is very cool and air-conditioned. Aside from that, the toilets arerelativelyv clean and there is an assortment of food, entertainment, and shopping as well scattered throughout the mall. It is also conveniently linked to the large Dhoby Ghaut MRT Interchange which makes this mall very accessible.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This shopping mall is highly convenient and offers an impressive variety of stores, making it a place where you can find almost anything you need. The directory includes a well-stocked supermarket, numerous fashion boutiques, electronics retailers, and a wide selection of dining options ranging from quick bites to sit-down restaurants. The layout is generally logical, though it can take some time to navigate due to the sheer size of the complex. It serves as an excellent one-stop destination for both daily necessities and specialty items. Facilities like restrooms and parking are well-maintained and accessible. Given the extensive range of shops and services available under one roof, it is a highly practical choice for weekend errands or casual browsing. However, because of its comprehensive nature, it tends to attract large crowds, especially during peak hours and weekends. Overall, it provides a highly efficient and reliable shopping experience for visitors looking to complete multiple tasks in a single trip.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: A nice, bustling shopping mall set over multiple levels. Lots of restaurants and takeaways across multiple levels, plus a large Kopitiam food court on Level 6. Besides the eateries and 'regular shops' there is a cinema, games arcades and various niche stores, plus access to the Dhoby Ghaut MRT station. Notably clean restrooms.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Plaza Singapura is very crowded but extremely useful. I had multiple skin and hair treatments here and got excellent results was 99.99.9% effective. You can trust the professionals here; they deliver exactly what they promise.😎

It’s sitting immediately on the right when you face the ISTANA.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Restaurant have several hit and miss cuisines but the most recent visit had me trying the fish and fries. It was really good and affordable. The fish was not overly battered and there were thick flakes of fish within that is light and fluffy!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Singapore Swimming Club has a great selection of Indian food and have always enjoyed the food, especially someone who is very discerning. Having said that, off late the quality of paneer dishes seems to be off. The paneer tikka is no longer soft, creamy and flavourful. I think they may have changed their supplier or switched to using frozen paneer. The management should look into it and bring back the original quality paneer.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Been coming here for years since I’ve been a kid. Always love how homely and family-friendly this place is. The eateries are fantastic, especially the cafe and Chinese restaurant. The club sports facilities are excellent, I’ve used the pool, tennis courts, bowling alley, and gym before. With the Katong Park station almost ready nearby, it will become even more accessible.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Went there for the second time as my aunt is their member. The first round tried their lunch buffet. Decent taste in many varieties. This round tried their fried hokkien mee. place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
This MRT theme is Old Singapore art paintings.

Take time to observe, we could see four to five old Singapore paintings showcased.

The one at  train level is the big art showing Sim Lim towers and old Auro Rickshaws. These are very heart touching and remembering our old Singapore life style.

This MRT has a small Backery and Cloth Store as well inside.

One thing must to say, we need to go down 5 levels to catch train means this is one of the deepest MRT in City.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The ramp / walkway leading up to the escalator is so dirty and looked like it have never been clean before with so many stains left for months or even longer than that. Upkeep of cleanliness here is 1 of the worst among most MRT stations. Passenger service is one of the worst here.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Singapore's MTR and transportation system is not a question at all. The train were quite new and well maintained, clean inside and fewer passengers. The MTR system map is also accessible online. Jalan Besar station were near cheaper hotels and asian cuisine. Please follow my fb at Cybercode Shan and Shan Cabillan on Tktok for more travel updates!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I alighted and later boarded here today (5 March 2026). Earlier, I alighted to visit the nextdoor via Exit A, Sim Lim Square. As I live in the Hillview area, this Downtown Line station is such a convenience to me to go to the Rochor area and Little India - that is, without having to change MRT line. I also noticed that this MRT Station is quite deep underground but getting to the ground level is facilitated by two sets of ascending (or descending) escalators. This MRT Station appears well maintained and clean.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
🟦 Rochor MRT station
11 Rochor Canal Road
● Singapore 188505

🟦 Rochor MRT Station (✅️ DT13)
*************************************
🔵 This is my first visit to Rochor MRT Station. ● This underground Downtown Line station features a spacious concourse and 2 main exits ( ➡🟧  Exit A and Exit B).

🟦 Exit A leads you to Rochor Canal Road, near Sim Lim Square, Burlington Square, Fu Lu Shou Complex, Albert Complex, LASALLE College of the Arts, and nearby shops and hotels. ✔️ It’s the busier primary exit with escalators & llifts.

🟦 Exit B opens toward Sungei Road and nearby residential blocks, Masjid Abdul Gafoor, Rochor Centre, Sim Lim Tower, and the vicinity of Tekka Place/Indian Heritage Centre ( ➡️ 🟡 Bus stop “Bef Rochor Station Exit B”).

🟡 I took Bus 57 from Blk 27 Toa Payoh East and alighted at “Bef Rochor Station Exit B” ----which is a short walk to the station entrance.

🟢 Overall, this is a well-connected and convenient station with good access to shopping, cultural spots and buses.

➡️ 6 Feb 2026 / Friday afternoon

If you're looking to shop for electronics, you'd likely need to pass through this MRT station, as it brings you directly to Sim Lim Square. The station is relatively elderly-friendly too, as there's Bencoolen Street nearby.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: This MRT station is within walking distance to many busy areas, situated just beside a few, namely Sim Lim Square to get electronics and devices, Laselle College of the Arts and opposite the Rochor stretch of malls, Tekka Place, Burlington Square Shopping Mall, Indian Heritage Centre. It is also near to Sim Lim Tower, NAFA campuses, OG Albert complex, Bencoolen mall and various hotels. The station’s internal is designed artistically. Overall nice and new appeal.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: I alighted and later boarded here today (5 March 2026). Earlier, I alighted to visit the nextdoor via Exit A, Sim Lim Square. As I live in the Hillview area, this Downtown Line station is such a convenience to me to go to the Rochor area and Little India - that is, without having to change MRT line. I also noticed that this MRT Station is quite deep underground but getting to the ground level is facilitated by two sets of ascending (or descending) escalators. This MRT Station appears well maintained and clean.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
🟦 Rochor MRT station
11 Rochor Canal Road
● Singapore 188505

🟦 Rochor MRT Station (✅️ DT13)
*************************************
🔵 This is my first visit to Rochor MRT Station. ● This underground Downtown Line station features a spacious concourse and 2 main exits ( ➡🟧  Exit A and Exit B).

🟦 Exit A leads you to Rochor Canal Road, near Sim Lim Square, Burlington Square, Fu Lu Shou Complex, Albert Complex, LASALLE College of the Arts, and nearby shops and hotels. ✔️ It’s the busier primary exit with escalators & llifts.

🟦 Exit B opens toward Sungei Road and nearby residential blocks, Masjid Abdul Gafoor, Rochor Centre, Sim Lim Tower, and the vicinity of Tekka Place/Indian Heritage Centre ( ➡️ 🟡 Bus stop “Bef Rochor Station Exit B”).

🟡 I took Bus 57 from Blk 27 Toa Payoh East and alighted at “Bef Rochor Station Exit B” ----which is a short walk to the station entrance.

🟢 Overall, this is a well-connected and convenient station with good access to shopping, cultural spots and buses.

➡️ 6 Feb 2026 / Friday afternoon

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I visited here for a morning walk. The park is quite large, so you'll definitely work up a sweat walking around. I highly recommend bringing a water bottle. It's full of nature, and spotting a wild chicken was a fun experience.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Relaxing spot, perfect for a nature retreat while in the city. A lot less crowded than you'd expect, considering it's central spot.

It's a collection of spots really, rather than a single destination, with lifestyle dining, museums, educational spice gardens, wedding photo destinations, or just a plain picnic spot. It's much less of a climb than you'd think, is great all around.

View from the top is a comforting one, nice to see the cityscape.

One interesting thing is how it's occupied mainly by maids, foreign workers, and tourists. As an aside, Chinese tourists and their themed outfits are kinda fun! Maybe we should look into that. Under-utilized by Singaporeans - thats your tax dollars at work you know, might as well use it.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a quiet green park in Singapore. Looks like an old park with lot of greenery. It's clam, quiet and with lot of shade its ideal for a relaxing time. The famous Fort canning tree tunnel is also located here. Entrance is free. Popular place amongst the tourists. Good place to relax.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Fort Canning Park is a lush green destination right in the heart of the city, notable for its tranquil atmosphere and historical significance. It's perfect for strolling, relaxing, and taking photos, especially with its famous "tree tunnel" viewpoint.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: A compact but lively local football ground with a real community feel. The Lion City Sailors ultras bring great energy and atmosphere, making the match experience feel personal and passionate. The stadium itself is on the smaller side, and the selection of food, jerseys, and activities is pretty limited, but it still delivers a solid, authentic local football vibe that’s easy to enjoy.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Played here before in the 80s during my club's local cup match. Everything change, now. Good field, spectacular event matches held here. Historical landmark Jalan Besar Stadium. 👍👍👍

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: A must visit for soccer die-hard fans. The place has changed a fair bit over the years -- but it still retains its old world appeal. Love it! Easy enough to find a car park with the next door multi-storey carpark facility. Loads of good food all around the stadium too!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Watched the historic match between Lion City Sailors and Hiroshima Sanfrecce here. The seats were close to the action which made for a great viewing experience.

However, it seems like the stadium/staff weren’t prepared for the large crowd (4000+ people). The toilets ran out of water so we couldn’t wash our hands, and the water coolers also ran out of water during halftime. Plus they did not allow people to bring water bottles in, forcing us to leave the water bottles on a table outside, but the table was not large enough to accommodate all bottles, by the end of the event the bottles were all placed on the floor instead. This led to a very long queue for drinks, which we managed to get prematch, but abandoned our halftime queue for second cups due to the super long wait. I get it that LCS isn’t used to attracting such large crowds, but they need to up their game for the upcoming semifinals, which is bound to attract an even larger crowd. The toilets need to have water, and there needs to be more drink vendors (otherwise, simply let people bring their own water if you can’t sell them water). Looking forward a better semifinals experience!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Easy to navigate your way here.
Nice atmosphere here for games.

The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Late lunch after a “Reunion Senior Friendly Tour” at National Museum of Singapore (NMS) on Sat 10 Jan 2026 morning. Remembered there was a “Y Cafe” at the 4th floor of the YMCA building next to NMS.

A quiet place with great window view of the sky, The Cathay and the School of the Arts (SOTA). A good place to enjoy an afternoon tea/coffee/drinks and catch up with friends.

Prices are reasonable. Taste of my fish & chips was mediocre. I don’t visit Orchard area often so I am not sure if we can get better tasting food at similar prices nearby. The view is great though.

Friendly and patient service staff who had his own late lunch after serving me.

A family of 4, two groups of friends in 2s or 3s and a solo visitor when I was at the cafe.

Some 12 table set up that can accommodate groups of 2-4 pax and a counter area with high seats that can sit 3-4 pax.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: The YMCA of Singapore is so much more than just a building; it’s a heartbeat for social good in the city. I’m consistently impressed by their dedication to youth development and their programs for the special needs community (like the Y-Stars). It’s a professional, inclusive, and transparent nonprofit that truly puts people first. Whether you’re looking to volunteer or support a great cause, this is the place!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Strategic location. Very clean rooms. Don’t have much to say except it’s a decent value for what you’re paying. Nothing fancy, but the internet is fast, the water is hot, sheets are clean, aircon is cold. There is a sizeable fitness center and the staff are helpful.  Would recommend.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
We stayed at the YMCA orchard road
They are the most amazing staff you will come across. The room was clean and spacious and the be was comfortable.
Public transport is on your doorstep for busses and  two train stations a couple of minutes walk away.
It's an a amazing central location to everything and don't miss satay street or you will regret it. Amazing food.
To get there walk out of arrivals hip on bus 36 and it will drop you at the door. When returning to the airport hop on the same bus for $3-6 and you go back to the airport hassle free.
My wife and I can't recommend this place enough it's amazing.
After a day of sightseeing come back to a beautiful air-conditioned room relax and you are ready to go again.
A big thank you to all the staff you are amazing people
Bob and Judy

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: I had a nice and pleasant stay at the YMCA. The staff was very friendly, offering me an early check-in and providing a room with a window as requested. The cleanliness, room quality, and location were excellent! For a 3-star hotel, the YMCA is truly a great deal.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As an artist working in the Singapore Sax Symposium 2026 (SSS 2026) at NAFA this June - it was such a smooth, well-run event by all the employees/student workers there!! The event (started originally by lecturer Dr Zechariah Goh) has also brought much value and recognition to NAFA within the local/regional saxophone scene for its contributions to the community over the years. I hope NAFA continues to acknowledge and hopefully increase its support of this event and the artists/workers involved (e.g. funding, manpower support) in the coming years.

Special shout out to Chew Kai-Xin (NAFA employee) who organised this event and led her team to success. In all my years of attending this event, this has been the smoothest and best experience that I’ve had. Speaking from someone who has first hand experience handling administrative/organisational roles, Kai-Xin and her team were professional, helpful, and totally competent in creating the best experience for artists and attendees alike. Will 10/10 recommend her to continue running this symposium and/or other projects at NAFA. She and her team deserve a raise!!!! 🎷😎👍

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I just attended a short course in "Making a Complete Picture Book with Procreate" with NAFA this year (2026).

It has been a great privilege to learn from a very experienced and professional Procreate Storybook Illustrator, Mr. Josef Lee. In this short span of 7 weeks (one lesson per week for 3 hours), he generously shared with us his storybook creation processes - his idea thumbnail sketches, design developments and thought process. It was truly an amazing experience as we are learning from a very committed storybook designer whose storybooks creations are actually published and can be found in many bookstores, national libraries and even a hotel in Singapore!

Mr. Josef Lee helped us to understand the rigour, the detailed planning, the workflow and the putting together of all the research and ideas to come up with a really enticing read for children and even adults. He was also very helpful in encouraging us to ask questions and answering them all. He respects our privacy and shy disposition as well and gave us one-to-one constructive feedback in our weekly beginner story-creating attempts.

In an overview, we had learnt how to come up with:
-  a good storyline and story structure
-  storyboarding (in Procreate)
-  characters design methods (in Procreate)
-  picture composition and layout (in Procreate)
-  colour palettes (in Procreate)
-  digital drawing, colouring and animating (in Procreate)
-  exporting our digital artworks into an eBook, and more...

He has also shared with us successful examples by other internationally renowned storybook creators. The takeaways that he shared from his journeys of constant storybook creations and readings to children are very meaningful...  such as - The lesser you draw, the harder your text will have to work to provide the information for your story.

The class size is intentionally kept small (max of 10) to ensure the high quality of students' learning. Thank you, for this decision is greatly appreciated and valued! I have also made many nice friends and laughed in class. Highly recommending this course 😊

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Reignited a long-forgotten passion through an 8-week oil painting course with NAFA CLE. It was a genuinely rewarding experience—guided by a wonderful trainer, Nhawfal Juma’at and shared with an enthusiastic group of classmates from all walks of life, all taking time to reconnect with their creative side. Thank you for creating such a warm and inspiring learning environment.

PS: Love the Spotify song list too! ")

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
Completed Beginner, Intermediate and Advanced Watercolour courses and it has been a truly rewarding and fulfilling learning journey. I only wish each level had more sessions because the time always felt too short!

One of the highlights was meeting people who share the same interest in watercolour. My classmates were incredibly supportive — we cheered each other on, shared experiences and often continued conversations over lunch or coffee after lessons. It made the learning experience even more enjoyable and motivating.

Our instructor Edwin Ho, is patient and supportive of our learning. He encourages us, while giving us challenges that push us out of our comfort zones, which really helped us grow and improve.

With the National Silver Academy (NSA) subsidy and the use of SkillsFuture credits, the courses are even more affordable for those aged 50 and above, which is a wonderful initiative.

Overall, a wonderful experience. We definitely need more watercolour sessions!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I attended the Nagomi pastel art classes, and our facilitator, Zu, was very encouraging and friendly. The classes were enjoyable, and her instructions were clear and easy to follow.
I’m giving one star less because the administrative support from NAFA could be improved. We were only informed of the materials list less than two weeks before the class start date, and as it was during the CNY period, many of us had to rush to get the items. The response time from the administration office was also rather slow and they could do better with support for their staff lah.

review_text:
As an artist working in the Singapore Sax Symposium 2026 (SSS 2026) at NAFA this June - it was such a smooth, well-run event by all the employees/student workers there!! place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I just attended a short course in "Making a Complete Picture Book with Procreate" with NAFA this year (2026).

It has been a great privilege to learn from a very experienced and professional Procreate Storybook Illustrator, Mr. Josef Lee. In this short span of 7 weeks (one lesson per week for 3 hours), he generously shared with us his storybook creation processes - his idea thumbnail sketches, design developments and thought process. It was truly an amazing experience as we are learning from a very committed storybook designer whose storybooks creations are actually published and can be found in many bookstores, national libraries and even a hotel in Singapore!

Mr. Josef Lee helped us to understand the rigour, the detailed planning, the workflow and the putting together of all the research and ideas to come up with a really enticing read for children and even adults. He was also very helpful in encouraging us to ask questions and answering them all. He respects our privacy and shy disposition as well and gave us one-to-one constructive feedback in our weekly beginner story-creating attempts.

In an overview, we had learnt how to come up with:
-  a good storyline and story structure
-  storyboarding (in Procreate)
-  characters design methods (in Procreate)
-  picture composition and layout (in Procreate)
-  colour palettes (in Procreate)
-  digital drawing, colouring and animating (in Procreate)
-  exporting our digital artworks into an eBook, and more...

He has also shared with us successful examples by other internationally renowned storybook creators. The takeaways that he shared from his journeys of constant storybook creations and readings to children are very meaningful...  such as - The lesser you draw, the harder your text will have to work to provide the information for your story.

The class size is intentionally kept small (max of 10) to ensure the high quality of students' learning. Thank you, for this decision is greatly appreciated and valued! I have also made many nice friends and laughed in class. Highly recommending this course 😊

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Farrer Park MRT Station (NE8) is a key stop along the North East Line, conveniently serving the vibrant Little India area. It is strategically located near residential buildings, hotels, shopping malls, and cultural landmarks, making it a frequently used station by both locals and tourists.

The station is clean, well-maintained, and designed with accessibility in mind, featuring lifts, escalators, and clear signages to assist commuters of all ages. Its direct connection to City Square Mall is a huge plus, providing easy access to shopping, dining, and other amenities without stepping outside, which is especially convenient during rainy weather.

However, despite its convenience and upkeep, the station’s platforms and walkways feel relatively narrow, particularly during peak hours. With the growing population and increasing foot traffic in the area, the station often feels cramped, causing congestion and slowing down the movement of commuters. This can be uncomfortable, especially for those in a hurry.

Expanding the station’s width would greatly enhance the overall commuting experience, allowing smoother crowd flow and ensuring the station can accommodate the rising number of daily passengers comfortably.

Farrer Park MRT Station remains a vital transportation hub, but improvements to its capacity and space would better serve the needs of today’s fast-paced and busy commuters.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: A convenient stop by Rangoon and Serangoon Road to get to the medical centers, hotels nearby and Mustafa in Little India.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Farrer Park MRT Station (NE8) is an underground station along the North East Line (NEL), located within the Farrer Park and adjacent to the Kampong Java and Lavender planning subzones. The station sits underneath the Connexion building, an integrated hospital and hotel complex.

Farrer Park was named after John Farrer, who was President of the Municipal Commissioners from 1919 to 1931. The area was renamed to Farrer Park in 1935 after the old Singapore Turf Club moved to Bukit Timah, leaving behind an old racecourse where Farrer Park Primary School now stands.

The station is located in the vicinity of residential apartments near Pek Kio and Tessenohn Road, shophouses and commercial / residential mixed developments near Owen Road and Rangoon Road, as well as condominiums and shopping centres near Kitchener Road.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Loved the MRT system on our trip to Singapore. Easy to navigate with a familiar tap and pay to use. Cheap as well.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Quite a few entrances which gives easy and sheltered accessibility to the surrounding areas.
Cool and not usually crowded.
This serves only one line though, NE or purple line.
There are no amenities there so one has to go all the way to City Square to go to the restroom or buy a drink.
Also , the taxi stand is quite far and a very quiet taxi stand which hardly anyone uses , except maybe on Sundays. So it is hard to get a taxi leaving from this station

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
We visited Maxwell Food Centre at around 8:00 AM on a Monday, so it was still relatively quiet with very few queues. Unfortunately, the famous chicken rice stall was closed, which was a bit disappointing. This was actually my second visit, and I still haven't had the chance to try it!

Thankfully, there are plenty of other stalls serving chicken rice, so we simply picked one without a line and it didn't disappoint. In fact, that's the beauty of Maxwell Food Centre. There are so many food options to choose from, and everything we tried was delicious. The drinks were great too, and the prices are very affordable. Those in the pictures are only some of those we found really delicious.

Whether you're craving local favorites or just looking for a good meal, Maxwell Food Centre never disappoints. If you're in Singapore, make sure to stop by for delicious food at budget-friendly prices. Just keep in mind that if you're hoping to try the famous chicken rice, it's worth checking the stall's opening hours before you go!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There’s a wide variety of food and the laksa was absolutely amazing. It’s cheaper if you pay cash instead of card. The staffs in charge of cleaning the trays were really proactive. We didn’t try the super crowded stalls, but the ones we had were pretty solid in terms of quality. Also no bad odor

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
I was here in the evening time and most of the stalls were closed for the day.
But I am very fortunate of finding a chicken rice shop still open.
Uncle Louis Famous chicken rice. Uncle was very hands on. He takes each customer's order attentively and gives suggestions along the way.
I ordered chicken with soup and veggies. Very tender, fresh veggie sides and hot rice. Yummy! Welcome local dinner on my 1st night in Singapore.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
A step back in time with fantastic food variety at really bargain prices. Only one of the 3 stall rows has high velocity fans for extra cooling. A few obvious winners among the vendors. We had Vietnamese  Tofu and noodles and vegetarian rice paper rolls.
A local character has found a way to always get his favorite seat - Xtra load rap music. He managed to get a whole table under a fan for himself during the lunch peak. There's someone who understands marketing!
A fantastic authentic street food location that has a busy lunch peak but is much quieter otherwise.
The lady selling fresh crushed fruit and sugar can drinks is a standout nearthe China Town exit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: My favourite eating place in the city by far. So much to choose from, foodie heaven. There's always a good selection of shops open. Can be very busy at time with queues for the most popular places.  Don't be scared to share tables. When finished, put your tray in designated area before leaving.
